finding: sync handler moved into ziee_framework::sync::routes: the tokio::select! loop (channel recv / recheck.tick / exp deadline), recheck_interval() incl the debug-only SYNC_RECHECK_TICK_MS seam, the ConnGuard drop-unregister, and the connected handshake are 1:1 with the former subscribe_sync. The four ziee concretes (ProfileRead gate, JwtService exp, Repos re-check, SyncConnPrincipal/registry/SyncSseEvent) are read through R:IdentityResolver + S:SyncSurface. ziee handlers.rs is now sync_routes::<ZieeIdentityResolver, SyncEntity>().

SyncSurface::recheck (ziee impl in event.rs) reproduces the former inline re-check byte-for-byte: Repos.user.get_by_id → is_active guard → admin?empty:get_user_groups → !admin && !check_permission_union(profile::read) => TearDown → else Refresh(SyncConnPrincipal); Ok(_)=>TearDown (deactivated/removed); Err(_)=>Transient (DB blip keeps the stream). The framework loop maps Refresh→registry().refresh, TearDown→break, Transient→no-op — identical to the three former match arms.

SSE auth-gate PRESERVED. Subscribe = RequirePermissions<R,S::BaselinePerms> (ziee (ProfileRead,) => profile::read), byte-identical gate. The 60s re-check still re-resolves is_active + the baseline perm and tears down on loss; the JWT exp still bounds the stream deadline (via IdentityResolver::access_token_exp, byte-identical extraction); the handshake carries only a connection id; wire is notify-and-refetch only (no row data). No audience/routing logic changed (that lives in the already-moved registry). Nothing weakened.

ziee_framework::sync::routes names NO ziee concrete: grep for SyncEntity/SyncSseEvent/SyncConnPrincipal/JwtService/Repos/check_permission_union/ProfileRead/crate::modules in routes.rs => EMPTY. Wire enum opaque as S::Wire (200 schema only), principal opaque as S::Principal, auth via R. The schema-bearing wire types + publish + registry() singleton correctly STAY in ziee. access_token_exp added to IdentityResolver with default None (non-breaking; ziee-auth unaffected).

Golden BOTH surfaces. subscribe_sync_docs (framework) hardcodes the same operation metadata (id Sync.subscribe, tag Sync, summary, description) + with_permission::<S::BaselinePerms> (=> profile::read extension) + .response::<200, Json<S::Wire>>() (S::Wire = ziee SyncSseEvent, schema keyed by short ident => ref unchanged). onboarding OpenAPI: OnboardingProgress key by short ident unchanged; route paths/perms unchanged. Regenerated ui+desktop: types.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL, openapi.json canonically-equal (jq -S) vs baseline; generated paths reverted via git checkout.

ziee-onboarding models.rs moved BYTE-FOR-BYTE (OnboardingProgress, 0 crate:: refs); repository.rs moved with ONE edit (crate::common::AppError → ziee_core::AppError, the same re-exported type). The 3 methods (get_progress query_as!, complete_guide/complete_guide_step query! with the atomic cardinality-cap upsert) are byte-identical. Handlers keep calling Repos.onboarding.* returning the same OnboardingProgress.

202607140195_onboarding_schema.sql (table, NO FK) moved BYTE-FOR-BYTE to the crate (filename+content => sqlx checksum+version preserved); 202607144185_onboarding_fkeys.sql (user_id→users(id)) stays in ziee module migrations. Added ziee-onboarding/migrations to server/build.rs sdk_crate_migration_dirs. The merged set version-sorts …0195 (table) before …4185 (FK, which sorts after users), so the MERGED schema is identical. Proven: app build.rs provisioned the merged build DB + cargo check -p ziee (onboarding query! via Repos + schema) exit 0; schema.fp UNCHANGED vs baseline.

ziee-onboarding/migrations applies on a bare DB: grep REFERENCES public. in the crate's migrations => EMPTY (user_id is a plain NOT NULL uuid, no FK). Declared crate-dep ziee-core ships no migrations, so the migration closure = its own one file. Added to standalone_apply_gate.sh CRATES; the crate's OWN build.rs already proves it (provision_build_db applies migrations/ alone to ziee_onboarding_build_<key> and the query! verify => cargo check -p ziee-onboarding exit 0).

ziee-framework gains async-stream/futures-util/chrono (versions matching the ziee server catalog => single src-app/Cargo.lock unifies). ziee-onboarding deps (ziee-core + serde/schemars/sqlx[postgres,uuid,macros]/uuid; build-dep ziee-build-support) version-match. onboarding/mod.rs pub use ziee_onboarding::{OnboardingRepository, models} keeps Repos.onboarding (core/repository.rs:50) + super::models::OnboardingProgress (handlers) resolving. sdk cargo check --workspace + ziee + ziee-desktop all exit 0.

cargo check -p ziee-desktop exit 0; desktop --generate-openapi => types.desktop.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL + openapi.desktop.json canonically-equal vs baseline. Desktop embeds the ziee lib (gets ziee_onboarding + the framework sync_routes transitively) and shares the merged migration set (its build.rs points at the same ziee_build_<key>), so both moved surfaces compose identically. sync_router() mounts the framework route on both surfaces.

onboarding handlers.rs (with the is_valid_onboarding_id #[cfg(test)] units — 4 tests) stays in ziee byte-unchanged. ziee-onboarding models/repository carry no #[cfg(test)] units => cargo test -p ziee-onboarding = 0 tests (compiles). The framework sync/registry TestEntity mock (SyncEntityKind, session_signal only) is UNTOUCHED (SyncSurface is a separate trait, not a widening of SyncEntityKind), so the registry unit tests still compile+pass. Integration sync::/onboarding:: drive the real endpoints (see TESTS-MOVED).

Deliverable 1: ziee's sync module surface is unchanged for callers — publish/publish_session_to_users/SyncEntity/SyncAction/Audience/SyncOrigin still re-exported from modules::sync; only handlers.rs internals changed (now a thin mount). Deliverable 2: onboarding/mod.rs re-exports keep every call site + Repos factory resolving with zero edits outside mod.rs+Cargo.toml+build.rs. Dropped the now-unused ClientConn/SYNC_CHANNEL_CAPACITY re-exports from ziee registry.rs (the moved handler consumed them) — no external consumer.
